channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon faces sentencing for threatening ex-Speaker McCarthy
item.description: A Montana man faces years in federal prison at his sentencing on Wednesday for threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Latvia FM: Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he couldn't in Ukraine war
item.description: Latvia’s foreign minister is warning that Russian President Vladimir Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he hasn’t been able to accomplish in his war against Ukraine — weaken the United States and restore control over his smaller neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Byron Donalds, backed by Trump, says he's running for Florida governor
item.description: Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds announced Tuesday that he's entering the 2026 race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, who is ineligible to seek another term after eight years in office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SEIU members demand better contract at Kern County Board of Supervisors meeting
item.description: The union is set to hold a one-day strike March 5, in hopes of bringing Kern County negotiators back to the bargaining table.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem announces DHS registry for illegal aliens in US that will require them to be fingerprinted
item.description: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem told illegal immigrants to either self-deport, register and be fingerprinted with the federal government or jeopardize their chance of freedom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP adopts Trump budget after topsy-turvy night
item.description: House Republicans adopted the budget resolution that will lay the foundation for enacting President Trump's legislative agenda Tuesday night, just minutes after they initially pulled the measure from the floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: White House confirms who was appointed as acting administrator of DOGE
item.description: White House officials told Fox News the acting administrator of DOGE is Amy Gleason despite billionaire Elon Musk overseeing the department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP, after pulling budget resolution, brings it back for a vote
item.description: House GOP leaders brought their budget resolution to the floor for the vote, just minutes after pulling it at the last minute Tuesday evening as they struggled to win over a handful of conservative fiscal hawks who had threatened to tank the framework for President Trump's ambitious legislative agen
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump says he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern border state governor takes on cartels and security with signing of executive order
item.description: Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs signed an executive order this week to establish a multi-agency task force to take on cartels and secure the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Upheaval and firings at CDC raise fears about disease outbreak response
item.description: Staff and observers worry that the agency may not be prepared for emerging threats including bird flu and insect-borne diseases.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Prosecutors conclude Army veteran's Capitol riot pardon also covers a separate weapons case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has concluded that a military veteran's presidential pardon for charges that he stormed the U.S. Capitol also extends to his separate conviction for illegally possessing stolen grenades and classified information, according to a court filing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How federal funding flows from the White House to Minnesotans
item.description: Amid the Trump administration’s slashes to program budgets from scientific research to education to social services, nonprofits and state agencies are left in financial and legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellen Finn, MPR News

item.title: Bondi warns three states to comply with law keeping boys out of girls sports or face legal action
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: Attorney General Pam Bondi is warning California, Maine and Minnesota to comply with the federal antidiscrimination laws that require them to keep boys out of women’s sports or face legal action, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump strips security clearances from law firm tied to Jack Smith cases
item.description: The White House said President Donald Trump will sign a memo suspending the security clearances of several employees of Covington & Burling LLP after they assisted Jack Smith's probes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rules against drivers in a case advocates say could make civil-rights claims harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Tuesday that people who win early rulings in civil rights cases won't necessarily be able to recover their legal fees, a finding that both conservative and liberal groups had argued could make it harder to fight for people's rights in court.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 40% of contracts canceled by Musk's DOGE are expected to produce no savings
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that President Donald Trump's administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: RYAN J. FOLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans have muscled their budget blueprint to passage, with a push from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal prisons prep to move trans inmates as early as next week
item.description: The Bureau of Prisons is moving forward with plans to move transgender inmates out of prisons that align with their gender identity and into facilities that align with their assigned sex at birth.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
